heading: National Urban Search and Rescue Response System
label: 5165f National Urban Search and Rescue Response System

children.heading: Administrator
children.label: 1 Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency.

children.heading: Agency
children.label: 2 Agency
children.text: The term “Agency” means the Federal Emergency Management Agency.

children.heading: Hazard
children.label: 3 Hazard
children.text: The term “hazard” has the meaning given the term in .section 5195a of this title

children.heading: Nonemployee System member
children.label: 4 Nonemployee System member
children.text: The term “nonemployee System member” means a System member not employed by a sponsoring agency or participating agency.

children.heading: Participating agency
children.label: 5 Participating agency
children.text: The term “participating agency” means a State or local government, nonprofit organization, or private organization that has executed an agreement with a sponsoring agency to participate in the System.

children.heading: Sponsoring agency
children.label: 6 Sponsoring agency
children.text: The term “sponsoring agency” means a State or local government that is the sponsor of a task force designated by the Administrator to participate in the System.

children.heading: System
children.label: 7 System
children.text: The term “System” means the National Urban Search and Rescue Response System to be administered under this section.

children.heading: System member
children.label: 8 System member
children.text: The term “System member” means an individual who is not a full-time employee of the Federal Government and who serves on a task force or on a System management or other technical team.

children.heading: Task force
children.label: 9 Task force
children.text: The term “task force” means an urban search and rescue team designated by the Administrator to participate in the System.

children.heading: General authority
children.label: b General authority
children.text: Subject to the requirements of this section, the Administrator shall continue to administer the emergency response system known as the National Urban Search and Rescue Response System.

children.heading: Functions
children.label: c Functions
children.text: In administering the System, the Administrator shall provide for a national network of standardized search and rescue resources to assist States and local governments in responding to hazards.

children.heading: Designation
children.label: 1 Designation
children.text: The Administrator shall designate task forces to participate in the System. The Administration shall determine the criteria for such participation.

children.heading: Sponsoring agencies
children.label: 2 Sponsoring agencies
children.text: Each task force shall have a sponsoring agency. The Administrator shall enter into an agreement with the sponsoring agency with respect to the participation of each task force in the System.

children.heading: Participating agencies
children.label: A Participating agencies
children.text: A task force may include, at the discretion of the sponsoring agency, one or more participating agencies. The sponsoring agency shall enter into an agreement with each participating agency with respect to the participation of the participating agency on the task force.

children.heading: Other individuals
children.label: B Other individuals
children.text: A task force may also include, at the discretion of the sponsoring agency, other individuals not otherwise associated with the sponsoring agency or a participating agency. The sponsoring agency of a task force may enter into a separate agreement with each such individual with respect to the participation of the individual on the task force.

children.heading: Management and technical teams
children.label: e Management and technical teams
children.text: The Administrator shall maintain such management teams and other technical teams as the Administrator determines are necessary to administer the System.

children.heading: Appointment of System members into Federal service
children.label: f Appointment of System members into Federal service

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator may appoint a System member into Federal service for a period of service to provide for the participation of the System member in exercises, preincident staging, major disaster and emergency response activities, and training events sponsored or sanctioned by the Administrator.

children.heading: Nonapplicability of certain civil service laws
children.label: 2 Nonapplicability of certain civil service laws
children.text: The Administrator may make appointments under paragraph (1) without regard to the provisions of title 5 governing appointments in the competitive service.

children.heading: Relationship to other authorities
children.label: 3 Relationship to other authorities
children.text: The authority of the Administrator to make appointments under this subsection shall not affect any other authority of the Administrator under this chapter.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 4 Limitation
children.text: A System member who is appointed into Federal service under paragraph (1) shall not be considered an employee of the United States for purposes other than those specifically set forth in this section.

children.label: A
children.text: to reimburse each employer of a System member on the task force for compensation paid by the employer to the System member for any period during which the System member is appointed into Federal service under subsection (f)(1); and

children.label: B
children.text: to make payments directly to a nonemployee System member on the task force for any period during which the nonemployee System member is appointed into Federal service under subsection (f)(1).

children.heading: Reimbursement for employees filling positions of System members
children.label: 2 Reimbursement for employees filling positions of System members

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to such terms and conditions as the Administrator may impose by regulation, the Administrator shall make payments to the sponsoring agency of a task force to be used to reimburse each employer of a System member on the task force for compensation paid by the employer to an employee filling a position normally filled by the System member for any period during which the System member is appointed into Federal service under subsection (f)(1).

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: Costs incurred by an employer shall be eligible for reimbursement under subparagraph (A) only to the extent that the costs are in excess of the costs that would have been incurred by the employer had the System member not been appointed into Federal service under subsection (f)(1).

children.heading: Method of payment
children.label: 3 Method of payment
children.text: A System member shall not be entitled to pay directly from the Agency for a period during which the System member is appointed into Federal Service under subsection (f)(1).

children.heading: Personal injury, illness, disability, or death
children.label: h Personal injury, illness, disability, or death

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A System member who is appointed into Federal service under subsection (f)(1) and who suffers personal injury, illness, disability, or death as a result of a personal injury sustained while acting in the scope of such appointment, shall, for the purposes of subchapter I of chapter 81 of title 5, be treated as though the member were an employee (as defined by section 8101 of that title) who had sustained the injury in the performance of duty.

children.label: i
children.text: receive benefits under such subchapter; or

children.label: ii
children.text: receive benefits from the State or local government.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: B Deadline
children.text: A System member or dependent shall make an election of benefits under subparagraph (A) not later than 1 year after the date of the personal injury, illness, disability, or death that is the reason for the benefits, or until such later date as the Secretary of Labor may allow for reasonable cause shown.

children.heading: Effect of election
children.label: C Effect of election
children.text: An election of benefits made under this paragraph is irrevocable unless otherwise provided by law.

children.heading: Reimbursement for State or local benefits
children.label: 3 Reimbursement for State or local benefits
children.text: Subject to such terms and conditions as the Administrator may impose by regulation, if a System member or dependent elects to receive benefits from a State or local government under paragraph (2)(A), the Administrator shall reimburse the State or local government for the value of the benefits.

children.heading: Public safety officer claims
children.label: 4 Public safety officer claims
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to bar any claim by, or with respect to, any System member who is a public safety officer, as defined in section 1204 of title I of the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 [], for any benefits authorized under part L of title I of that Act ( et seq.).34 U.S.C. 1028442 U.S.C. 37961See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Liability
children.label: i Liability
children.text: A System member appointed into Federal service under subsection (f)(1), while acting within the scope of the appointment, shall be considered to be an employee of the Federal Government under and chapter 171 of that title, relating to tort claims procedure.section 1346(b) of title 28

children.heading: Service
children.label: 1 Service
children.text: Service as a System member shall be considered to be “service in the uniformed services” for purposes of chapter 43 of title 38 relating to employment and reemployment rights of individuals who have performed service in the uniformed services (regardless of whether the individual receives compensation for such participation). All rights and obligations of such persons and procedures for assistance, enforcement, and investigation shall be as provided for in such chapter.

children.heading: Preclusion
children.label: 2 Preclusion
children.text: Preclusion of giving notice of service by necessity of appointment under this section shall be considered to be preclusion by “military necessity” for purposes of pertaining to giving notice of absence from a position of employment. A determination of such necessity shall be made by the Administrator and shall not be subject to judicial review.section 4312(b) of title 38

children.heading: Licenses and permits
children.label: k Licenses and permits
children.text: If a System member holds a valid license, certificate, or other permit issued by any State or other governmental jurisdiction evidencing the member’s qualifications in any professional, mechanical, or other skill or type of assistance required by the System, the System member is deemed to be performing a Federal activity when rendering aid involving such skill or assistance during a period of appointment into Federal service under subsection (f)(1).
Training and exercises, including training and exercises with other Federal, State, and local government response entities.
Acquisition and maintenance of equipment, including interoperable communications and personal protective equipment.
Medical monitoring required for responder safety and health in anticipation of and following a major disaster, emergency, or other hazard, as determined by the Administrator.

children.heading: Response cooperative agreements
children.label: m Response cooperative agreements
children.text: The Administrator shall enter into a response cooperative agreement with each sponsoring agency, as appropriate, under which the Administrator agrees to reimburse the sponsoring agency for costs incurred by the sponsoring agency in responding to a major disaster or emergency.

children.heading: Obligations
children.label: n Obligations
children.text: The Administrator may incur all necessary obligations consistent with this section in order to ensure the effectiveness of the System.

children.heading: Equipment maintenance and replacement
children.label: o Equipment maintenance and replacement
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Administrator shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees (as defined in ) a report on the development of a plan, including implementation steps and timeframes, to finance, maintain, and replace System equipment.December 16, 2016section 101 of title 6

children.heading: Federal employees
children.label: p Federal employees
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to mean that a task force may not include Federal employees. In the case of a Federal employee detailed to a task force, the sponsoring agency shall enter into an agreement with the relevant employing Federal agency.
